entities.name: Breach of Warranty of Habitability
entities.description: A claim that the landlord has failed to maintain residential premises and common areas in a condition fit for human habitation, free from dangerous, hazardous, or detrimental conditions to life, health, or safety.

entities.name: Condition of Premises
entities.description: Evidence proving the existence of conditions that make the premises unfit for human habitation or dangerous to life, health, or safety.
entities.source_quote: the premises so leased or rented and all areas used in connection therewith in common with other tenants or residents are fit for human habitation... and that the occupants of such premises shall not be subjected to any conditions which would be dangerous, hazardous or detrimental to their life, health or safety.

entities.name: Tenant's Non-Causation
entities.description: Evidence that the condition was not caused by the misconduct of the tenant or persons under their direction or control.
entities.source_quote: When any such condition has been caused by the misconduct of the tenant or lessee or persons under his direction or control, it shall not constitute a breach of such covenants and warranties.

entities.name: Court Action for Damages
entities.description: A tenant initiates a lawsuit in court to recover damages for breach of the warranty of habitability.

steps: Present evidence of conditions and causation

entities.name: Damages Award
entities.description: The court awards monetary damages to the tenant for the landlord's breach of the warranty of habitability.

entities.name: Rent Reduction Offset
entities.description: For rent-regulated units, the court must reduce the damages award by the amount of any rent reduction previously ordered by DHCR for the same conditions.

entities.name: Warranty of Habitability Statute
entities.description: Establishes an implied warranty in all residential leases that premises are fit for human habitation and free from dangerous conditions.

entities.name: Local Emergency Housing Rent Control Law
entities.description: Cited as a rent regulation law; damages under RPL 235-b must be offset by DHCR rent reductions ordered under this law.

type_specific_fields.citation: Local Emergency Housing Rent Control Law

entities.name: Emergency Tenant Protection Act of 1974
entities.description: Cited as a rent regulation law; damages under RPL 235-b must be offset by DHCR rent reductions ordered under this act.

entities.name: Rent Stabilization Law of 1969
entities.description: Cited as a rent regulation law; damages under RPL 235-b must be offset by DHCR rent reductions ordered under this law.

entities.name: City Rent and Rehabilitation Law
entities.description: Cited as a rent regulation law; damages under RPL 235-b must be offset by DHCR rent reductions ordered under this law.
